title: Box Frame Console Table
summary: A simple 2x2 box frame base topped with a single 1x12 board — glued raw-to-raw so the top actually bonds instead of just sitting on a finished surface.
description:
The base is nothing more than two ladder-style side frames joined by short connectors, but the finishing sequence is the one detail worth following exactly: finish the base before attaching the top if you want a two-tone look, but leave the top edge of the frame completely raw. Stain or paint on that surface would act as a release layer between the glue and the wood, weakening the bond between the base and the top exactly where it needs to be strongest.

A generous bead of glue across the whole frame top, not just at the corners, is what keeps a single-board top like this from cupping or separating from the base over time — a few screws alone aren't enough grip across that much surface area.

materials.name: Pine, 2x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Furring strips are a cheaper substitute.

materials.name: Self-tapping trim screws, 1-5/8", or 1-1/4" brad nails

cutList.note: Or 3" less than the actual width of your 1x12 top.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four legs at 29-1/4", four side frame pieces at 45" and four end frame pieces at 8-1/4" from the Pine. Cut one top at 48" from the Pine. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to four 8 ft Pine and one 8 ft Pine. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the two side frames
steps.body: Build two ladder-style side frames from the legs and side frame pieces, using 2-1/2" screws — one screw per joint with generous glue.

steps.title: Connect the frames
steps.body:
Connect the two side frames with the shorter end frame pieces, attaching with glue and 2-1/2" screws. Set the assembled frame on a level surface and adjust any joints as needed so it sits flat without rocking. Let the glue fully dry.

If you want a two-tone finish, paint or stain the base now, before attaching the top — but leave the top edge of the frame completely raw. Finish there would act as a release layer and weaken the glue bond with the top.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body:
Apply glue generously across the whole top edge of the frame — not just at the corners. Set the 1x12 top in place and nail or screw down.

Fill any visible nail or screw holes with wood filler, let dry, and sand lightly. Finish the top with stain and a clear polyurethane coat to seal it.

images.alt: Simple box frame console table with a single-board top
